title: 9 Herbal Tinctures to Help Alleviate Dizziness Symptoms

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Bilobalide is good for dizziness because it has a calming effect on the brain, helps to reduce anxiety and stress, and improves blood flow to the brain, which can alleviate symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.
Quercetin is good for dizziness because it helps to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help to alleviate symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.
Kaempferol may help with dizziness because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can improve blood circulation and reduce the risk of vertigo.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture can help with dizziness due to its ability to improve blood flow to the brain. When blood flow to the brain is poor, it can cause dizziness and lightheadedness. Ginkgo biloba tincture contains flavonoids and terpenoids that help to widen blood vessels and improve circulation. This increased blood flow helps to deliver more oxygen and nutrients to the brain, which can help to reduce symptoms of dizziness. Ginkgo biloba tincture may also help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to dizziness. Additionally, it may help to improve memory and cognitive function, which can also contribute to feelings of dizziness and disorientation. Some people find that taking Ginkgo biloba tincture helps to reduce their dizziness and improve their overall sense of balance and stability.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a tincture from Piper methysticum, also known as kava, which can help with dizziness, you will need a few basic items. Start by gathering 1-2 cups of dried kava root, which can be purchased online or at a local health food store. You will also need a clean glass jar with a lid, a solvent such as high-proof vodka or glycerin, and a cheesecloth or a coffee filter. To begin, combine the dried kava root and the solvent in the glass jar, making sure the kava is completely covered by the liquid. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the solvent extract the active compounds from the kava. After the extraction period, strain the mixture through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. You now have a tincture that can be taken orally by placing a few drops under the tongue, allowing the active compounds to be absorbed quickly into the bloodstream. Begin with a small dose and gradually increase as needed to help alleviate dizziness.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for dizziness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for dizziness because it helps to calm the brain and nervous system, which in turn reduces the sensation of dizziness and promotes a sense of balance.
Valeranone is good for dizziness because it helps to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of dizziness and nausea by acting as a natural sedative and stabilizer.
Isovaleric acid, also known as kava, helps alleviate dizziness by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can help stabilize blood pressure and improve balance.
Valerenal helps with dizziness because it has a calming effect on the brain and body, reducing feelings of nausea and vertigo caused by stress and anxiety.
Valerenol is good for dizziness because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles, which can help to stabilize the inner ear and balance system.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a natural remedy that can help with dizziness. The tincture, which is made from the root of the plant, has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. When taken internally, the active compounds in valerian root, such as valerenic acid and valepotriates, can help to calm the brain and central nervous system, reducing feelings of dizziness and vertigo. Valerian root tincture can also help to regulate the body's internal balance and equilibrium, which is often disrupted when experiencing dizziness. Additionally, valerian root has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with dizziness. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, valerian root tincture can help to alleviate symptoms of dizziness and provide relief from dizziness-related discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper methysticum herbal tincture, also known as kava, which helps with dizziness, you will need some dried kava root, a jar with a lid, a saucepan, a strainer, and a glass bottle. First, place one cup of dried kava root in a saucepan and add two cups of water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Next, remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for 30 minutes to 1 hour. Strain the liquid through a strainer into a jar, discarding the solids. Add vodka or another type of high-proof alcohol to the liquid in a ratio of 1:1, so if you have 2 cups of liquid, add 2 cups of vodka. Stir the mixture well and let it sit for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every few days. After the steeping time, strain the liquid again through a strainer into a glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your kava tincture is now ready to use. Take a few drops under the tongue as needed to help with dizziness.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for dizziness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Shogaols are good for dizziness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the inner ear, which is often the cause of dizziness and vertigo.
Gingerols, also known as Kava, are good for dizziness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of lightheadedness and disorientation.
Zingiberene is good for dizziness because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the inner ear and improve balance.

Curcumenol in PIPER METHYSTICUM is good for dizziness because it helps to relax the brain and nervous system, which can reduce the feeling of dizziness and lightheadedness by promoting balance and calmness.
Bisabolol in PIPER METHYSTICUM helps to reduce dizziness by calming the nervous system and improving blood flow to the brain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tincture is made from ginger, a natural herb that can help with dizziness. When you feel dizzy, it's usually because your body is having trouble balancing its blood flow and balance. Ginger in the tincture helps to relax the blood vessels in your body, allowing blood to flow more smoothly and evenly. This can help to reduce the feeling of dizziness and lightheadedness. Additionally, ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm your stomach, which can sometimes cause dizziness. By reducing nausea and stomach discomfort, ginger tincture can help to alleviate dizziness and make you feel more balanced and steady.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Piper methysticum herbal tincture that helps with dizziness, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by gathering dried Piper methysticum roots or leaves from a trusted supplier, and fill a clean glass jar with about one cup of the dried herb. Next, pour a high-proof vodka or ethanol over the herb, making sure that the liquid completely covers the plant material. The general ratio is about 1 part herb to 2 parts liquid, but this can be adjusted to your preference. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the herb infuse into the liquid. After the infusion time has passed, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. You now have a concentrated Piper methysticum tincture that can be taken as needed to help alleviate dizziness.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for dizziness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins in PIPER METHYSTICUM are good for dizziness because they help calm the nervous system and improve blood flow to the brain, which can reduce feelings of dizziness and lightheadedness.
Avenacoside may help prevent dizziness by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the brain, which helps to stabilize the body's balance and equilibrium.
Fisetin is good for dizziness because it helps to calm the nerves and improve blood flow to the brain, which can reduce feelings of dizziness and lightheadedness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tincture is made from the oat plant and is often used to help with dizziness. This is because it can calm the nervous system and help the body feel more balanced. The tincture contains a type of chemical called avenacoside, which can help to reduce feelings of dizziness and lightheadedness. It works by slowing down the activity of the brain and nervous system, which can become overactive and cause dizziness. When taken, the tincture can help to calm the body and mind, reducing feelings of dizziness and helping the body to stay grounded. Avena sativa tincture is often used in small doses to help alleviate symptoms of dizziness, and it can be taken at any time when dizziness is a problem.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Piper methysticum herbal tincture to help with dizziness, start by gathering dried Piper methysticum roots or leaves. You can purchase these from a health food store or online. Next, combine one part of the dried Piper methysticum with one part of a solvent like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. The ratio of herb to solvent is important, so use a 1:1 ratio to get the best results. Secure the lid tightly and store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herb release its properties into the solvent. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Piper methysticum tincture is now ready to use, typically taken in small amounts (5-10 drops) under the tongue or added to water.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal tinctures for dizziness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids in PIPER METHYSTICUM are good for dizziness because they help to strengthen blood vessels and improve blood circulation, which reduces the likelihood of dizziness caused by low blood pressure.

Kaempferol, commonly known as Kava, may help reduce dizziness due to its ability to increase the activity of certain neurotransmitters, such as GABA and acetylcholine, which helps to calm the nervous system and promote a sense of balance and stability.
Quercetin is good for dizziness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can cause dizziness and balance issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as Passionflower, is a natural remedy used to help with dizziness. The tincture is made from the plant's leaves and flowers, which contain a unique combination of chemicals that calm the nervous system. When taken, these chemicals slow down brain activity and help to balance the body's internal equilibrium. This leads to a decrease in feelings of dizziness and vertigo. People who suffer from dizziness due to anxiety or stress may find relief with Passiflora incarnata tincture, as it helps to calm the mind and body. In addition, Passionflower has been traditionally used to treat insomnia and restlessness, which are often linked to dizziness. As a result, the tincture may also help people sleep better at night, further alleviating symptoms of dizziness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a tincture of Piper methysticum, also known as kava, which can help with dizziness, you will need some dried kava roots, a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like coconut oil, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, fill the jar about 2/3 full with the dried kava roots. Then, pour the solvent over the kava, making sure that all of the roots are covered. The general ratio for this mixture is about 1 part dried kava to 2 parts solvent. Next, seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for about 2-3 weeks. During this time, the kava will extract into the solvent, creating the tincture. After the extraction period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, add the carrier oil to the tincture, about 1 part carrier oil to 1 part solvent, and mix well. This kava tincture can be taken in small amounts, usually 1/4 teaspoon to 1 teaspoon, mixed with water or another liquid, to help alleviate dizziness.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal tinctures for dizziness

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a piper methysticum tincture that helps with dizziness, you will need dried kava root, a clean glass jar with a lid, a measuring cup, a mixing cup, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, prepare your kava root by cleaning and drying it properly. Then, measure out one part of dried kava root and add it to your glass jar, followed by two parts of your chosen solvent, making sure the kava is completely covered. Next, place the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the kava root release its active ingredients into the solvent. After the steeping period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, pressing on the solids to get as much liquid out as possible. Discard the solids and store your tincture in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, mix a few drops with water and drink it as needed to help alleviate dizziness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax quinquefolius tincture is made from the American ginseng plant and is often used to help with dizziness. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper methysticum herbal tinctures that help with dizziness, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, you will need to gather a handful of dried Piper methysticum roots or leaves, which can be found in health food stores or online. Next, you will need to combine the Piper methysticum with a neutral solvent such as vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar, making sure that the plant material is completely covered by the solvent. The general ratio for this mixture is 1 part Piper methysticum to 2 parts solvent, but you can adjust this to your liking. remedies.remedy_desc: Piper methysticum, also known as kava, is a plant-based remedy that has been used for centuries to help with dizziness. The tincture made from its root is believed to calm the nerves and relax the muscles, which can help to reduce dizziness. This is because kava contains compounds that can slow down the activity of the nervous system, which can become overactive and cause dizziness. When taken as a tincture, these compounds can be absorbed quickly into the body, providing fast relief from dizziness and other symptoms. Kava tincture may also help to reduce anxiety, which is often a cause of dizziness. As the body becomes more relaxed and calm, the symptoms of dizziness can subside, allowing the person to feel more stable and balanced.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper methysticum herbal tincture that helps with dizziness, you will need some dried Piper methysticum leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a dark glass dropper bottle. First, measure out a handful of dried Piper methysticum leaves and place them in the glass jar. Next, pour enough solvent over the leaves to cover them completely, making sure the leaves are fully submerged. Stir the mixture well, then seal the jar with a lid and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process is called maceration, which helps to extract the beneficial properties from the leaves into the solvent. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass dropper bottle, discarding the solids. Your Piper methysticum tincture is now ready to use - simply take a few drops as needed to help alleviate dizziness.
remedies.image_alt: Piper methysticum herbal tinctures for dizziness

intro_desc: Dizziness can be a distressing and debilitating symptom that affects many people. It can make everyday activities, like walking or even standing up, a challenging and potentially hazardous task. Fixing the issue is crucial because dizziness can lead to injuries, accidents, and even long-term health problems. One effective way to alleviate dizziness is through herbal tinctures, which are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs that can be taken sublingually (under the tongue).  Ginkgo biloba is a popular herb used to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, which can help alleviate dizziness caused by poor circulation or lack of oxygen. It's also known to improve memory and cognitive function, making it an excellent choice for people experiencing dizziness related to age-related decline or neurological conditions.  Valeriana officinalis, on the other hand, is a natural sedative and anti-anxiety herb that can help calm the nervous system and reduce dizziness caused by stress, anxiety, or insomnia. It promotes relaxation and balance, making it an excellent choice for people experiencing dizziness due to emotional or psychological factors.  By taking herbal tinctures like Ginkgo biloba and Valeriana officinalis, you can improve your overall well-being and reduce the frequency and severity of dizziness episodes. This can allow you to resume your normal activities, enjoy your daily routine, and live a more balanced and fulfilling life. Improved balance and reduced dizziness can also reduce the risk of accidents and injuries, giving you peace of mind and a sense of security.
supplementary_best: For dizziness, a combination of Ginkgo biloba, Ginger (Zingiber officinale), and Avena sativa may be beneficial. Ginkgo biloba is known to improve blood flow to the brain, helping to stabilize blood pressure and reduce dizziness. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nausea and stomach discomfort often associated with dizziness. Avena sativa, also known as oat straw, is a natural relaxant that can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which can be a contributing factor to dizziness.  This combination works well because it addresses multiple aspects of dizziness. Ginkgo biloba targets the physical aspect by improving blood flow, while Ginger addresses the digestive aspect by reducing nausea. Avena sativa, on the other hand, targets the emotional aspect by calming the nervous system and reducing anxiety. By combining these three herbs, you can effectively address the various factors that contribute to dizziness.  When taking this combination, it's essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed. It's also crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.
supplementary_avoid: If you experience dizziness, it's best to avoid certain herbal tinctures that may worsen the condition. One such herb is lobelia (Lobelia inflata), which can cause dizziness, lightheadedness, and even fainting due to its stimulating effect on the nervous system. Another herb to avoid is yohimbe (Pausinystalia johimbe), which can cause blood pressure to rise, potentially exacerbating dizziness. Additionally, tinctures made from pennyroyal (Mentha pulegium) and wormwood (Artemisia absinthium) should be avoided, as they can cause dizziness and even seizures due to their toxic compounds. Furthermore, skullcap (Scutellaria lateriflora) can cause drowsiness and lightheadedness in some individuals, which may worsen dizziness. It's also essential to be cautious with tinctures made from black cohosh (Actaea racemosa) and goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis), as they can cause dizziness and nausea in some people. If you're prone to dizziness, it's best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger is a natural remedy that can help with dizziness. It has properties that help calm the stomach and reduce nausea, which is often a cause of dizziness. Ginger can be eaten raw, cooked in food, or made into a tea. When you eat ginger, it sends a message to your brain to slow down your digestive system, which helps your body get the nutrients it needs without feeling dizzy.  Cold or warm compresses on the neck can also help with dizziness. When you apply a compress to your neck, it can help improve blood flow to your brain, which can help you feel more balanced. This is because the blood vessels in your neck are close to your brain, so when you apply pressure to them, it can help increase blood flow to your brain and reduce dizziness.  Resting in a quiet, dark room can also help with dizziness. When you're in a quiet, dark room, your body doesn't have to work as hard to process the information it's getting from your environment. This can help your body and brain recover from the dizziness, and it can help you feel more calm and balanced.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience dizziness often have other health issues that can be treated with medicinal herbs. One common problem is low blood pressure, which can be caused by a lack of iron in the blood. Herbs like ginseng and ashwagandha can help increase blood pressure by strengthening the heart and improving circulation.  Another issue is fatigue, which can be caused by anemia or a lack of oxygen in the blood. Herbs like rhodiola and schisandra can help increase energy levels by improving the body's ability to use oxygen and reducing stress.  Some people with dizziness also have digestive problems, such as nausea or stomach ulcers. Herbs like peppermint and licorice root can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea.  In addition, people with dizziness may experience anxiety or stress, which can make dizziness worse. Herbs like passionflower and valerian root can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety.  People with dizziness may also have a problem with their inner ear, which is responsible for balance. Herbs like ginkgo biloba and ginger can help improve blood flow to the inner ear and reduce dizziness.  Lastly, some people with dizziness may have a problem with their vitamin B12 levels, which is important for nerve function. Herbs like holy basil and skullcap can help improve nerve function and reduce dizziness.  These herbs can be used in various forms, such as teas, capsules, or tinctures, and should be consulted with a healthcare professional before use.
